channel.title: Weekly on Martin Proks (@mproksik)
channel.description: Recent content in Weekly on Martin Proks (@mproksik)

item.title: Weekly news #17
item.description: News Ubuntu 21.04 is out Science/Bioinformatics Machine-learning-based dynamic-importance sampling for adaptive multiscale simulations Bhatia et al., Nat Mach Intell (2021) #machine-learning #sampling Multiscale simulations are a well-accepted way to bridge the length and time scales required for scientific studies with the solution accuracy achievable through available computational resources. Traditional approaches either solve a coarse model with selective refinement or coerce a detailed model into faster sampling, both of which have limitations.

item.title: Weekly news #16
item.description: News COSMIC to Arrive in June Release of Pop!_OS 21.04 Science/Bioinformatics Single-cell CUT&Tag profiles histone modifications and transcription factors in complex tissues Bartosovic et al., Nat Biotechnol (2021) #cut-and-tag #single-cell In contrast to single-cell approaches for measuring gene expression and DNA accessibility, single-cell methods for analyzing histone modifications are limited by low sensitivity and throughput. Here, we combine the CUT&Tag technology, developed to measure bulk histone modifications, with droplet-based single-cell library preparation to produce high-quality single-cell data on chromatin modifications.

item.title: Weekly news #15
item.description: News Announcing the 2021 Research Scholar Program Recipients MIT 18.S191/6.S083/22.S092 - Introduction to Computational Thinking Elon Musk startup shows monkey with brain chip implants playing video game Data Hut™ — Open Source Project Directory Science/Bioinformatics Entropy subspace separation-based clustering for noise reduction (ENCORE) of scRNA-seq data Song et al., Nucleic Acids Research (2021) #clustering #entropy Single-cell RNA sequencing enables us to characterize the cellular heterogeneity in single cell resolution with the help of cell type identification algorithms.

item.title: Bi-Weekly news #13 and #14
item.description: News Richard Stallman is Back! Gnome 40 is out with some major UI changes Science/Bioinformatics Abel Prize celebrates union of mathematics and computer science Davide Castelvecchi #abel-prize #mathematics Two pioneers of the theory of computation have won the 2021 Abel Prize, one of the most prestigious honours in mathematics. An Overview of Next-Generation Sequencing Athina Gkazi, PhD #ngs #overview Over the last 56 years, researchers have been developing methods and technologies to assist in the determination of nucleic acid sequences in biological samples.

item.title: Weekly news #12
item.description: News Git 2.31 CutiePi tablet Science/Bioinformatics Modelling human blastocysts by reprogramming fibroblasts into iBlastoids Liu et al., Nature (2021) #blastocyst #human #scrna-seq Human pluripotent and trophoblast stem cells have been essential alternatives to blastocysts for understanding early human development1,2,3,4. However, these simple culture systems lack the complexity to adequately model the spatiotemporal cellular and molecular dynamics that occur during early embryonic development. Here we describe the reprogramming of fibroblasts into in vitro three-dimensional models of the human blastocyst, termed iBlastoids.

item.title: Weekly news #11
item.description: News htsget-rs in GSoC 2021 7-zip officialy on Linux Science/Bioinformatics R.ROSETTA: an interpretable machine learning framework Garbulowski et al., BMC Bioinformatics (2021) #machine-learning #roseta We present the R.ROSETTA package, which is an R wrapper of ROSETTA framework. The original ROSETTA functions have been improved and adapted to the R programming environment. The package allows for building and analyzing non-linear interpretable machine learning models. R.ROSETTA gathers combinatorial statistics via rule-based modelling for accessible and transparent results, well-suited for adoption within the greater scientific community.

item.title: Weekly news #10
item.description: News Brave buys a search engine, promises no tracking, no profiling – and may even offer a paid-for, no-ad version R Conferences for 2021 Google Fonts Episode 1: Talking open source, bioinformatics and maple syrup with Phil Ewels of SciLifeLab Science/Bioinformatics ArchR is a scalable software package for integrative single-cell chromatin accessibility analysis Granja et al., Nat Genet 2021 #scrna-seq #scatac-seq The advent of single-cell chromatin accessibility profiling has accelerated the ability to map gene regulatory landscapes but has outpaced the development of scalable software to rapidly extract biological meaning from these data.

item.title: Weekly news #9
item.description: News EMBL International PhD Programme 2021 open for registration Science/Bioinformatics Accurate feature selection improves single-cell RNA-seq cell clustering Su et al.,Briefings in Bioinformatics 2021 #scrna-seq #feature-selection #clustering Cell clustering is one of the most important and commonly performed tasks in single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) data analysis. An important step in cell clustering is to select a subset of genes (referred to as ‘features’), whose expression patterns will then be used for downstream clustering.

item.title: Weekly news #8
item.description: News Mars Perseverance Photo Booth Huston, the Rover landed on Mars! Science/Bioinformatics Blame the Data Ten simple rules for getting started with command-line bioinformatics Brandies et al., PLoS Comput Biol 2021 #command-line Robust decomposition of cell type mixtures in spatial transcriptomics Cable et al., Nat Biotechnol 2021 #spatial-transcriptomics _A limitation of spatial transcriptomics technologies is that individual measurements may contain contributions from multiple cells, hindering the discovery of cell-type-specific spatial patterns of localization and expression.

item.title: Weekly news #7
item.description: News Stanford Makes iPhone Development Course Free Rust gets its own foundation Science/Bioinformatics The next 20 years of human genomics must be more equitable and more open DOI: 10.1038/d41586-021-00328-0 Brain2Pix: Fully convolutional naturalistic video reconstruction from brain activity Le et al., biorvix 2021 #neural-network #fmri #image-reconstruction Reconstructing complex and dynamic visual perception from brain activity remains a major challenge in machine learning applications to neuroscience. Here we present a new method for reconstructing naturalistic images and videos from very large single-participant functional magnetic resonance data that leverages the recent success of image-to-image transformation networks.

item.title: Weekly news #6
item.description: News explosion/spaCy v3 pip dropping support for python 2 Shiny 1.6 dplyr 1.0.4 homebrew 3.0 Science/Bioinformatics Initialization is critical for preserving global data structure in both t-SNE and UMAP Kobak et al., 2021 Nat Biotechnol #scrna-seq #dimension-reduction Five reasons why researchers should learn to love the command line Jeffrey M. Perkel #command-line The text interface is intimidating, but can save researchers from mundane computing tasks. Just be sure you know what you’re doing.

item.title: Weekly news #5
item.description: News Announcing the 2020 Golden Kitty Award Winners Postgres broken on Mac OS Big Sur Science/Bioinformatics Seven myths of how transcription factors read the cis-regulatory code Zeitlinger et al., Current Opinion 2021 #transcription-factors #enhancers Genomics data are now being generated at large quantities, of exquisite high resolution and from single cells. They offer a unique opportunity to develop powerful machine learning algorithms, including neural networks, to uncover the rules of the cis-regulatory code.

item.title: Weekly news #4
item.description: News RStudio 1.4 is out 🎉 Raspberry Pi Pico now on sale at $4 IPFS Support in Brave browser Science/Bioinformatics Ten computer codes that transformed science Jeffrey M. Perkel From Fortran to arXiv.org, these advances in programming and platforms sent biology, climate science and physics into warp speed. Noninvasive Precision Screening of Prostate Cancer by Urinary Multimarker Sensor and Artificial Intelligence Analysis Hojun et al., ACS Nano, 2021

item.title: Weekly news #3
item.description: News Nature Computational Science issue #1 is out 🎉 Pirated Academic Database Sci-Hub Is Now on the ‘Uncensorable Web’ Science/Bioinformatics Lessons from the COVID-19 pandemic for advancing computational drug repurposing strategies Galindez et al., Nat Comput Sci, 2021 #covid-19 #drug-disvocery Responding quickly to unknown pathogens is crucial to stop uncontrolled spread of diseases that lead to epidemics, such as the novel coronavirus, and to keep protective measures at a level that causes as little social and economic harm as possible.

item.title: Weekly news #2
item.description: Science/Bioinformatics DeepCell Kiosk: scaling deep learning–enabled cellular image analysis with Kubernetes Bannon et al., Nat Methods, 2021 #image-analysis #kubernetes #google-cloud 📋New software DeepCell Kiosk is designed to scale predefined deep-learning image analysis on Google cloud using Kubernetes. Deep learning is transforming the analysis of biological images, but applying these models to large datasets remains challenging. Here we describe the DeepCell Kiosk, cloud-native software that dynamically scales deep learning workflows to accommodate large imaging datasets.

item.title: Weekly news #1
item.description: Design patterns using Rust Are you a Rust fan? Did you know that there is an open source book where people contribute their code on how to implement various design patterns in Rust? Go check it out, the community already reached 4.2k stars on GitHub! Docker best practices We all use docker these days, but “with great power comes great responsibility” - Uncle Ben. Do you follow some security rules when you write your own Dockerfile?
